---
title: "Future Hydropower — High-Emitting Biomes"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 150)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(maps)
library(scales)
library(sf)
```

```{r load}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# FHReD future dams (same source as 14; use sheet 2 if sheet 1 is intro)
xl_path <- here("data/FHReD_2015_future_dams_Zarfl_et_al_beta_version/FHReD_2015_future_dams_Zarfl_et_al_beta_version.xlsx")
future <- read_xlsx(xl_path, sheet = 2)

# readd in grand data
grand_path <- "~/Desktop/MEDS/EDS-240-data-viz/eds240-infographic/data/GRanD_Version_1_3/GRanD_dams_v1_3.shp"
grand_raw <- st_read(grand_path)

names(grand_raw)
unique(grand_raw$PURPOSES)  # or use View() to inspect
# Li & He (2022) Table S2 — median carbon intensity by biome (kg CO2e/MWh)
biome_ci <- tribble(
  ~biome,     ~ci_median,
  "Tundra",      43.8,
  "Boreal",     104.4,
  "Temperate",   54.8,
  "Tropical",    91.0
)

# Assign biome from latitude (Li & He–style bands)
assign_biome <- function(lat) {
  case_when(
    lat >= 60 | lat <= -60 ~ "Tundra",
    (lat >= 50 & lat < 60) | (lat <= -50 & lat > -60) ~ "Boreal",
    (lat >= 23 & lat < 50) | (lat <= -23 & lat > -50) ~ "Temperate",
    lat > -23 & lat < 23 ~ "Tropical",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

future_geo <- future %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Capacity (MW)`), !is.na(LAT_cleaned), !is.na(Lon_Cleaned)) %>%
  mutate(
    biome = assign_biome(LAT_cleaned),
    basin = coalesce(`Major Basin`, "Other")
  ) %>%
  left_join(biome_ci, by = "biome")

# Basin-level totals (MW) and top basins for discrete coloring (ordered by size)
basin_mw <- future_geo %>%
  group_by(basin) %>%
  summarise(basin_mw_total = sum(`Capacity (MW)`), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(basin_mw_total)) %>%
  mutate(
    rank = row_number(),
    basin_ranked = if_else(rank <= 10,
                           paste0(rank, ". ", basin),
                           "Other basins")
  )

future_geo <- future_geo %>%
  left_join(basin_mw, by = "basin") %>%
  mutate(
    basin_group = factor(basin_ranked,
                         levels = c(
                           paste0(1:10, ". ", basin_mw$basin[1:10]),
                           "Other basins"
                         ))
  )

# Lat-band rectangles for background (biome zones)
# Tundra >60, Boreal 50–60, Temperate 23–50, Tropical -23–23, Temperate -23–-50, Boreal -50–-60, Tundra <-60
lat_bands <- tribble(
  ~ymin, ~ymax, ~biome,
  -90,   -60,   "Tundra",
  -60,   -50,   "Boreal",
  -50,   -23,   "Temperate",
  -23,    23,   "Tropical",
   23,    50,   "Temperate",
   50,    60,   "Boreal",
   60,    90,   "Tundra"
) %>%
  left_join(biome_ci, by = "biome")

world <- map_data("world")

# Colors: low CI = cooler (blue), high CI = warmer (amber/red). 80 kg = target line.
pal_ci <- c(
  "Tundra"    = "#639fab",   # low
  "Boreal"    = "#c45c2a",   # high
  "Temperate" = "#1c5d99",   # mid-low
  "Tropical"  = "hotpink"   # high
)

# Discrete palette for top 10 basins (+ other), following the ordered factor
pal_basin10 <- c(
  RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(10, "Paired"),
  "#666666"
)
```

```{r map}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
ggplot() +
  # Latitude bands by biome (emission intensity)
  geom_rect(
    data = lat_bands,
    aes(xmin = -180, xmax = 180, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = biome),
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_ci, name = "Biome (Li & He)") +
  # World outline
  geom_polygon(
    data = world,
    aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = "#f8f8f8", color = "#cccccc", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  # Future dam locations, colored by biome, size by capacity
  geom_point(
    data = future_geo,
    aes(x = Lon_Cleaned, y = LAT_cleaned, size = `Capacity (MW)`, color = basin_group,
        alpha = basin_group),
    shape = 16
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1.2, 11), labels = comma, name = "Capacity (MW)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_basin10, name = "Top future basins") +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c(rep(0.9, 10), 0.4),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 0.9, size = 3)),
    color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 0.85, size = 3))
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.3, xlim = c(-180, 180), ylim = c(-60, 75)) +
  labs(
    title = "Future hydropower is concentrated in high‑emitting biomes",
    subtitle = "Background = latitude biome (Li & He). Points = planned dams; color = top 10 basins by total future capacity (others in gray); size = individual dam capacity. Tropical & boreal median CI > 80 kg CO₂e/MWh.",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r legend-table, include=TRUE}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Legend: biome and median CI for clarity
biome_ci %>%
  mutate(
    ci_label = paste0(ci_median, " kg CO₂e/MWh"),
    above_80 = if_else(ci_median > 80, "> 80 (high)", "≤ 80")
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Biome", "Median CI", "vs 80 kg target"),
    align = c("l", "r", "l")
  )
```

**Takeaway:** Most planned capacity sits in Tropical and Temperate bands. Li & He (2022) report higher median carbon intensity in Tropical and Boreal biomes; siting in these zones implies higher emissions per MWh unless design and management reduce them.

**Data:** Dam locations and capacity from FHReD (Zarfl et al. 2015). Biome from latitude; median CI from Li & He (2022) Table S2.
